metadata.methodology: Discourse analysis applying framing analysis (Entman), discourse theory (Laclau/Mouffe), and DHA (Wodak/Krzyżanowski)
metadata.sources: Party programs, riksdag motions 2024/25–2025/26, party websites, parliamentary debate protocols
academic_references: Holt & Bolin (2025) – Resilient populism? SD discourse in parliament 2010–2024
Elgenius & Rydgren (2026) – Who are the People and what does Home mean?
Krzyżanowski & Ekström (2023/2025) – Saying Criminality, meaning Immigration
Schclarek Mulinari (2025) – Sweden's race to the bottom
Calvo, Bäck & Carroll (2024) – Debating the Populist Pariah
Gustafsson (2024) – Vad gör språket i politiken?
Fredén, Johansson & Saynova (2024) – Word embeddings on ideology from Swedish parliamentarians
metadata.scope: Fas 1 pilot: SD, S, V on rättsväsende and migration

trygghet.description: Central contested concept in Swedish politics — same word, fundamentally different meaning per party

SD.meaning: Physical safety from crime + cultural/ethnic belonging
SD.example: Återskapa tryggheten (through punishment and reduced immigration)

S.meaning: Hybrid — shifting from social welfare security toward physical safety from crime
S.example: Ett tryggare Sverige (identical campaign slogan to M)

V.meaning: Social and economic security as crime prevention
V.example: Trygghetszoner (resource investment, not repression) vs government's visitationszoner

trygghet.analytical_note: The concept has been captured. The original social-democratic definition (trygghet = work + housing + welfare) has eroded. SD and M have successfully redistributed the concept's meaning toward fear of crime. S has partially followed. V defends the original definition from an increasingly marginal position. Research reference: Tham (Dagens Arena) documents this shift in riksdag motions over time.

ansvar.description: Responsibility — who bears it and for what differs fundamentally between parties

SD.meaning: Individual responsibility to assimilate + previous governments' culpability for 'ansvarslös invandringspolitik'

S.meaning: Society's responsibility to intervene + individual's responsibility to integrate
S.example: Gemensamt ansvar / höga förväntningar på var och en

V.meaning: State's responsibility for equality + global responsibility for refugees
V.example: EU-länder måste ta sitt ansvar (for refugee reception)

ansvar.analytical_note: SD uses 'ansvar' primarily as accusation (others were irresponsible) and as demand (immigrants must take responsibility). S uses it bidirectionally — society and individual both carry responsibility. V directs it upward — toward the state, toward wealthy nations, toward structural power. The word is identical; the direction of obligation is opposite.

gemenskap.description: Community/belonging — the basis for social cohesion differs radically

SD.meaning: Ethno-cultural community based on shared culture, history, language, and national identity
SD.example: Samhörighet med det samhälle vi lever i (via nationalstaten as prerequisite)

S.meaning: Civic community based on participation, integration, and shared institutions

V.meaning: Class-based solidarity across ethnic and national boundaries
V.example: Solidaritet och gemenskap (against racism and nationalism)

gemenskap.analytical_note: SD's gemenskap requires cultural homogeneity — the nation-state is the necessary container. S's samhällsgemenskap is formally civic but has shifted toward demanding cultural adaptation. V's gemenskap is built on class solidarity and is explicitly anti-nationalist. The concept maps directly onto the GAL-TAN axis: GAL = universalist solidarity, TAN = particularist belonging.

discourse_profile.master_frame: Sweden is in decline due to mass immigration and failed left-liberal policies; restoration requires cultural homogeneity, strict law enforcement, and return migration
rhetorical_strategies: Nostalgia and golden-age rhetoric (Sverige ska bli bra igen)
Securitization of migration and cultural diversity
Affective contrast and ridiculing opponents (saft, bullar och straffrabatter)

galtan_congruence.explanation: Discourse is consistently TAN across all dimensions: authoritarian (harsh punishment, surveillance, indefinite detention), nationalist (crime and social problems attributed to immigration), traditionalist (victim's moral restitution over rehabilitation). Language matches ideological position closely.

rattsvasende.framing: Crime is a culturally imported problem — a direct consequence of mass immigration. The causal chain is: immigration → gang culture → insecurity. The solution is punishment, deportation, and immigration reduction, not social reform.

key_signifiers.meaning: Moral legitimation of repressive policy — the victim's perspective is framed as incompatible with rehabilitation
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Rehabilitation and victim justice are zero-sum; prioritizing one means neglecting the other

key_signifiers.meaning: Punishment that the offender genuinely feels — contrasted with what is framed as a lenient system
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: The current system is too soft; leniency is the cause of continued crime

key_signifiers.meaning: Law-abiding majority separated from criminals/illegals — creates an in-group/out-group binary
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: There is a clear line between 'ordinary people' and criminals; the state should protect the former from the latter

key_signifiers.meaning: Neighborhoods described as beyond repair due to 'socialist and liberal policies'
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Some areas are irredeemable and must be demolished — securitization at its most extreme

rhetorical_strategies.example: Sverige ska bli bra igen
Brottslingar ska få kännbara straff – inte saft, bullar och straffrabatter
Det nya rättsväsendet är hårt mot de hårda, men inte därför att man känner hat utan därför att man älskar det som man skyddar
Områden så förstörda av socialistisk och borgerlig politik att de helt enkelt måste rivas

rattsvasende.galtan_congruence: High TAN congruence. Authoritarian (surveillance, indefinite detention, reduced civil liberties for suspects), nationalist (links crime causally to immigration), moralist (punishment has intrinsic moral value). Rhetoric matches position.
rattsvasende.contrast_with: S shares some repressive vocabulary but retains structural causal analysis. V represents the polar opposite — crime as class problem, rehabilitation over punishment.

migration.framing: Migration is an existential threat to the nation-state. The nation requires cultural homogeneity for social trust and cohesion. Migration is not framed as an individual rights question but as a question of national survival.

key_signifiers.meaning: SD's most distinctive concept — normalizes the idea that people with residence permits should leave Sweden
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: People who have not culturally assimilated do not belong, even if legally present

key_signifiers.meaning: Consistently used to signal unmanageable volumes — frames all post-1990s immigration as excessive
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Any significant non-European immigration is by definition 'mass immigration'

key_signifiers.meaning: Imported cultures as burden rather than resource
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Cultural diversity weakens rather than enriches society

key_signifiers.meaning: Colloquial proverb signaling one-way assimilation, not mutual integration
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Swedish culture is the norm; newcomers must fully adapt

key_signifiers.meaning: Repeated phrase creating categories of people who by definition do not belong
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Presence in Sweden is a privilege, not a right, revocable for broad categories of non-citizens

rhetorical_strategies.example: Sverige har blivit en magnet för världens migranter. Detta på grund av ett generöst bidragssystem.
Migrationskartan visualizes demographic change per municipality to make immigration visually threatening through numbers
Återvandring är en human lösning — persons who have lived in Sweden a long time 'may feel greater belonging to another nation'
Målsättningen ska vara ett volymmässigt nettominus — frames population decrease as policy goal

migration.galtan_congruence: Strong TAN. Nationalist (ethno-cultural nation-state as ideal), authoritarian (state control over who may reside), assimilationist (one-way cultural adaptation). Fully congruent with GAL-TAN position.
migration.contrast_with: S has adopted 'stram' vocabulary but retains asylum rights in principle. V is the polar opposite — migration as human right, open borders as aspiration.

discourse_profile.master_frame: Sweden faces a national crisis requiring the state to 'step forward' — combining traditional social-democratic structural analysis with a new repressive layer on crime and restrictive stance on migration
rhetorical_strategies: Dual framing (brotten OCH brottens orsaker — but concrete proposals lean repressive)
Retroactive self-criticism (2015 as proof that strict policy is needed)
EU normalization (Sweden should not differ from other EU countries)

galtan_congruence.explanation: S occupies a discursive hybrid position. The party has retained GAL elements (structural causes, welfare, equality) but layered TAN vocabulary on top (skärpta straff, stram migrationspolitik, nationell kris). The rhetoric has moved toward Tidöpartierna faster than the formal policy position. The gap between S's self-image (center-left, welfare party) and actual discourse (increasingly repressive, restrictive) is the most analytically interesting finding.

rattsvasende.framing: Hybrid framing: crime has structural causes (inequality, segregation) AND requires repressive response (harsher sentences, surveillance, maffialag). The structural analysis is retained grammatically but subordinated in practice — concrete proposals cluster on the repressive side.

key_signifiers.meaning: Has shifted from welfare-security toward physical safety from crime — now overlaps with M's usage
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Trygghet is primarily about not being a victim of crime, not about economic security

key_signifiers.meaning: Reformulated from welfare commitment to security commitment — citizens are owed protection from crime
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: The social contract's primary obligation is safety, not equality

key_signifiers.meaning: State intervention — but now primarily meaning repressive intervention rather than welfare expansion
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: The state has lost control; regaining it requires force, not just resources

key_signifiers.meaning: Crisis framing that justifies exceptional measures
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Normal political tools are insufficient; extraordinary action is needed

rhetorical_strategies.example: Vi ska bekämpa brotten, men också brottens orsaker — but specific proposals are: maffialag, fotboja, straffskärpningar
Repression med precision
Vi befinner oss i en långvarig nationell kris — justifies measures beyond normal politics
S's recent justice minister 'boasted about having toughened sentences more than any other justice minister' — noted by Tham as marking a paradigm break
rhetorical_strategies.source: Dagens Arena analysis of S criminal policy shift

rattsvasende.galtan_congruence: Partial — sliding toward TAN. S's criminal policy discourse now shares more vocabulary with M and Tidöpartierna than with V. The structural analysis persists in subordinate clauses but is no longer the organizing frame. Research (Tham) documents this as a paradigm shift from the party's historical position.
rattsvasende.contrast_with: SD frames crime as immigration problem (cultural); V frames it as class problem (structural). S tries to hold both frames simultaneously but the repressive frame is gaining dominance.

migration.framing: Regulated, sustainable, strict migration within EU norms. Bureaucratic-technocratic language rather than rights-based. Asylum rights mentioned but subordinated to system sustainability. The 2025 party program formally commits to 'stram' migration policy 'under överskådlig tid'.

key_signifiers.meaning: Control and order — signals manageability without SD's antagonistic charge
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Previous immigration was insufficiently regulated; control is the priority

key_signifiers.meaning: New key term from 2025 party program — borrowed from right-of-center vocabulary
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Current levels remain too high; restriction is necessary

key_signifiers.meaning: Borrowed from sustainability discourse — implies current migration is unsustainable
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Migration at previous levels is incompatible with welfare state maintenance

key_signifiers.meaning: Civic community requiring active integration — close to SD's 'gemenskap' but without ethnic undertone
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Newcomers must adapt to Swedish norms; community requires cultural convergence

rhetorical_strategies.example: Det blev tydligt under flyktingkrisen 2014–2015 — S references its own time in power as proof that strict policy is needed
Vår migrationslagstiftning ska inte skiljer sig väsentligen från övriga länders i EU — makes restriction normal rather than extreme
Asylum rights are mentioned briefly but the discourse centers on system management, not individual rights. Compare V where asylrätten is the organizing nodal point.
rhetorical_strategies.source: Comparison of S vs V migration policy pages

migration.galtan_congruence: Discursive slide is clearer here than in criminal policy. S has adopted key terms (stram, hållbar, reglerad) that position it closer to center-right than to V. The traditional social-democratic migration rhetoric (solidarity, internationalism) survives only in subordinate clauses. The 2025 party program makes this shift programmatic.
migration.contrast_with: SD frames migration as existential threat; V frames it as human right. S occupies a technocratic middle ground but has drifted toward SD's framing of migration as primarily a problem to be managed.

discourse_profile.master_frame: Social problems — crime, insecurity, exclusion — are consequences of class inequality and dismantled welfare; the solution is redistribution, strong public services, and defense of human rights against authoritarian drift
rhetorical_strategies: Explicit counter-discourse (names and rejects the dominant frame)
Research legitimation (cites evidence against punitive approaches)
Gender analysis as differentiator (aggressiv maskulinitet, feministisk migrationspolitik)
Welfare-nostalgic counter-narrative (Sweden was safe because of welfare, not homogeneity)
Historical moral parallels (Holocaust, Balkan wars as precedent for asylum defense)

galtan_congruence.explanation: Discourse is consistently GAL across all dimensions: libertarian (opposes surveillance, mandatory detention, expanded police powers), universalist (rights-based, internationalist), progressive (feminist analysis, reparative justice). Language matches ideological position closely. V is the most discursively coherent party in the pilot — no gap between rhetoric and GAL-TAN placement.

rattsvasende.framing: Crime is a class problem — consequence of economic inequality, segregation, and dismantled welfare. The causal chain is: class inequality → segregation → lack of opportunity → gang recruitment. The solution is structural: strengthen welfare, reduce inequality, invest in prevention. Punishment is accepted for specific grave crimes but rejected as general strategy.

key_signifiers.meaning: V's nodal point — everything is related back to economic inequality
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Crime is ultimately a product of material conditions, not cultural factors or individual moral failure

key_signifiers.meaning: V's alternative to government's visitationszoner — same root word, opposite meaning. Resource investment instead of police powers.
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Safety comes from community investment, not from surveillance and search powers

key_signifiers.meaning: Justice through repair rather than punishment — frames victim support as compatible with offender rehabilitation
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Victim interests and offender rehabilitation are not zero-sum (contrasts with SD's framing)

key_signifiers.meaning: V's alternative label for what SD calls 'förstörda områden' and police call 'utsatta områden' — shifts blame to political neglect
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: These neighborhoods are not broken by their residents but by political decisions

rhetorical_strategies.example: Istället för politikers prat om längre och hårdare straff, en gemensam politisk vilja för att minska klassklyftorna
Forskning visar att längre och hårdare straff generellt sett inte får människor att sluta begå brott. Man kan se på USA som ett exempel.
En gemensam nämnare för de män som dras till våldsbejakande miljöer och gängkriminalitet är kvinnohatet — unique among riksdag parties
Sverige var länge ett land med lägre kriminalitet — en direkt koppling till att vi haft ett starkt välfärdssamhälle

rattsvasende.galtan_congruence: High GAL congruence. Structural/class analysis, rights-based, anti-authoritarian (opposes surveillance expansion, security zones, anonymous witnesses), feminist analysis. V is the most internally consistent party — no gap between discourse and GAL-TAN placement.
rattsvasende.contrast_with: SD attributes crime to immigration (cultural framing); S is hybrid but leaning repressive. V alone maintains the structural/class framing as its primary organizing principle.

migration.framing: Migration is a human right and a consequence of global inequality. The organizing principle is asylrätten as non-negotiable. Migration is 'natural and self-evident' — the opposite of SD's threat framing. Restrictions are framed as walls and barriers that cause suffering, not as rational policy tools.

key_signifiers.meaning: V's absolute nodal point in migration — non-negotiable, under attack, must be defended
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Asylum is a fundamental human right that overrides state sovereignty concerns

key_signifiers.meaning: Deliberate combination of idealism and pragmatism to counter accusations of naivety
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Solidarity and realism are compatible; it is the restrictive position that is unrealistic

key_signifiers.meaning: Physical metaphor for border restrictions — frames them as brutal barriers causing death
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Restrictions kill; openness saves

key_signifiers.meaning: Migration analyzed through gender lens — unique in Swedish debate
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Migration policy that ignores gender reproduces patriarchal structures

key_signifiers.meaning: Migration as normal human phenomenon — the opposite of SD's framing as crisis/threat
key_signifiers.implicit_assumption: Human movement is natural; it is borders and restrictions that are the aberration

rhetorical_strategies.example: From failed international attempts to save Jews from the Holocaust in 1938 to today's failures — draws direct line from history's greatest moral failure to current migration policy
V consistently uses 'människor på flykt', 'barn', 'kvinnor som är särskilt utsatta' — giving migrants agency and individuality, contrasting SD's deagentification (volumes, magnets, numbers)
I en tid då starka nationalistiska och högerextrema vindar blåser, måste vi som tror på mänskliga rättigheter fortsätta att försvara flyktingkonventionen
När patriarkala strukturer och bristande jämställdhet används som slagträ i debatten för att misstänkliggöra flyktingar skadar det arbetet för jämställdheten

migration.galtan_congruence: Strong GAL. Internationalist (open borders aspiration, EU solidarity), universalist (human rights as organizing principle), feminist (gender analysis of migration policy), anti-authoritarian (opposes border militarization, Frontex, surveillance). Fully congruent with GAL-TAN position.
migration.contrast_with: SD frames migration as existential threat requiring exclusion; S frames it as manageable through technocratic restriction. V alone frames migration as a human right requiring defense against authoritarian drift.
